title: Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR)
summary: You're logged in as alice and can view your invoices by id — but the server never checks the invoice is yours. Each stage adds a stronger but still-bypassable guard, until real per-object authorization.
goal: You're alice, shown only your own invoices — but bob and carol have accounts too. Read an invoice that isn't yours (bob's #102 is a $250,000 bonus).
goalSecure: Try the earlier tricks — reaching another holder's invoice (e.g. bob's #102) should be denied on every route, while your own (#101, #104) still load.
rootCause: The server fetches an object by an id taken from the request without verifying that the authenticated user is allowed to access that object — so changing the id reaches other users' data.
lessons: Authorize every object access against the authenticated user — fetching by id is not permission to read it.
Unpredictable identifiers are not access control: obscurity (random/encoded ids) only delays enumeration, it never authorizes.
Enforce the check on every path to the object — a forgotten export/print/API route leaks just as well as the main one.
Centralize the authorization check so it can't be skipped on one route; deny by default.

stages.title: Direct id, no check
stages.defense: None — any invoice id is returned.

stages.title: Check on the page, not the export
stages.defense: Ownership checked on the main route.
